body.title: agent_datacenter: Discord device — push-only v1, restart=reconnect
body.result: DiscordBotDevice implemented in agent_datacenter/devices/discord_bot/device.py — push-only v1, health via discord.log recency. 114 contract tests pass. Commit 3850610.
body.description:
Discord device — push-only in v1. Igor can send to Discord; Discord does not send to Igor (no inbound in v1). Restart semantics: reconnect the bot and re-authenticate.

Proposed shape:
- agent_datacenter/devices/discord_bot/device.py:
  class DiscordDevice(BaseDevice):
    capabilities() → {'push_only': True, 'pull': False, 'read_write': False}
    comms() → {'write_only': True, 'push': True, 'pull': False, 'nudge': False}
    health() → {'bot_connected': bool, 'authenticated': bool, 'latency_ms': float}
    restart() → shim.restart() = reconnect + re-authenticate
    where_and_how() → {'token_env': 'DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN', 'guild': guild_id, 'channel': channel_id}
    Send interface: DiscordDevice.send(channel_id, message) → sends via Discord API

Affected files:
- ~/dev/src/agent_datacenter/agent_datacenter/devices/discord_bot/device.py

Design rules:
- oop-first: DiscordDevice class; send() method — not a module-level function
- docs-in-code: 'push-only v1' note in class docstring with pointer to future inbound ticket

Scope boundary:
IN: push-only DiscordDevice implementing full BaseDevice contract
OUT: inbound message reading (v2), Discord slash commands (v2)

Test plan:
- DiscordDevice() instantiates (all abstract methods implemented)
- capabilities()['push_only'] == True
- health() returns {bot_connected: False} when DISCORD_BOT_TOKEN not set (no exception)
- restart() calls shim.restart() which does reconnect, not process kill
